title: crataegus oxyacantha subsp. monogyna
intro: Crataegus oxyacantha subsp. monogyna, also known as the common hawthorn, is a subspecies of the hawthorn tree that is native to Europe, North Africa, and Western Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, as well as its ability to lower blood pressure and improve cardiovascular health. The health benefits of Crataegus oxyacantha subsp. monogyna include reducing the risk of heart disease, improving lipid profiles, and alleviating symptoms of anxiety and insomnia. Herbal preparations of the plant include teas, tinctures, and capsules, which are often used to promote heart health and support overall well-being.
